metadata.title: A Survey of Approaches to Automated Manuscript Typesetting, with an Extended Discussion of Formatting Fidelity, Reproducibility, and Long-Form Bibliographic Practice in Engineering Venues
metadata.abstract: This example stresses two edge cases: (1) in-text citations using the [CITE n] anchor syntax, which resolve to IEEE bracket citations linked to a longer reference list, and (2) bullet and numbered lists mixed with body text. It also includes a deliberately long figure caption to check caption wrapping and pagination behavior near a column boundary.
metadata.conference: 2026 Example Symposium on Scholarly Publishing Tools

blocks.text: Prior systems have approached document typesetting from different angles. Some rely on template engines [CITE 1], others on constraint-based layout solvers [CITE 2], and still others on direct manipulation of Office Open XML structures [CITE 3]. This paper's compiler follows the template-engine tradition described in [CITE 1] but adds deterministic pagination.
A comparison of the three approaches is summarized as a list below.

items: Template engines: fast, but limited to pre-defined slots [CITE 1].
Constraint solvers: flexible, but computationally expensive [CITE 2].
Direct XML manipulation: precise, but brittle across Word versions [CITE 3].

blocks.text: The pipeline proceeds in the following numbered steps.

items: Parse the author's structured input into the JSON document model.
Resolve all [CITE n] anchors against the reference list, as described in [CITE 4].
Render each block deterministically using the Golden IEEE template.
Run preflight checks for image DPI, page count, and overflow.

blocks.text: An unusually long figure caption is used below to verify that captions wrap correctly within a single column and do not overlap adjacent content, even when the caption text approaches three full lines at the default caption font size, which is a common failure mode noted in earlier layout tooling [CITE 5].

blocks.caption: An example figure with an intentionally long caption used to test caption wrapping behavior: this text is meant to span multiple lines within the column width so that pagination and spacing near the caption can be verified against the two-column IEEE layout, including cases where the figure sits near the bottom of a column.

blocks.text: Citation numbering renumbers automatically if references are reordered in the source form, since [CITE n] anchors are resolved at compile time rather than typed as literal numbers.
This example demonstrates citation resolution, mixed list styles, and long-caption handling in a single compile.
